--- title: Two-Factor ANOVA subtitle: Sample Problems # author: Guenther, Benjamin A. project: type: website output-dir: docs format: html: html-math-method: mathjax self-contained-math: false embed-resources: false jupyter: python3 --- # About the factorial ANOVA problems use the `FactorialData` class which requires: * a tuple to specify the levels of each factor - (factor A, factor B) * the sample size per group - all groups will have the same sample size Each of the problems below sets factor levels randomly with 2 or 3 levels. Per-group sample size is randomly set between 5 and 15. An example funciton call is included below ::: {#d417f450 .cell execution_count=2} ``` {.python .cell-code} levels_factor_a = random.randint(2,3) levels_factor_b = random.randint(2,3) n = random.randint(5,15) FactorialData(design = (levels_factor_a, levels_factor_b), group_n = n).factorial_ANOVA() ``` ::: # Problem 1 Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with $\alpha = {0.01}$ to analyze the following data


Full Group Summary Data
$T_{A1B1} = {164} \quad M_{A1B1} = {20.5}\quad SS_{A1B1} = {38.0} \quad n_{A1B1} = {8}$
$T_{A1B2} = {209} \quad M_{A1B2} = {26.12}\quad SS_{A1B2} = {94.88} \quad n_{A1B2} = {8}$
$T_{A1B3} = {212} \quad M_{A1B3} = {26.5}\quad SS_{A1B3} = {86.0} \quad n_{A1B3} = {8}$
$T_{A2B1} = {241} \quad M_{A2B1} = {30.12}\quad SS_{A2B1} = {34.88} \quad n_{A2B1} = {8}$
$T_{A2B2} = {253} \quad M_{A2B2} = {31.62}\quad SS_{A2B2} = {57.88} \quad n_{A2B2} = {8}$
$T_{A2B3} = {262} \quad M_{A2B3} = {32.75}\quad SS_{A2B3} = {49.5} \quad n_{A2B3} = {8}$
$T_{A3B1} = {236} \quad M_{A3B1} = {29.5}\quad SS_{A3B1} = {34.0} \quad n_{A3B1} = {8}$
$T_{A3B2} = {181} \quad M_{A3B2} = {22.62}\quad SS_{A3B2} = {61.88} \quad n_{A3B2} = {8}$
$T_{A3B3} = {177} \quad M_{A3B3} = {22.12}\quad SS_{A3B3} = {28.88} \quad n_{A3B3} = {8}$
$\Sigma X^2 = {53741}$
$G = {1935}$

Stage 1 ANOVA Calculations

Calculate the Degrees of Freedom: $df_{total}, df_{within}, \text{ and } df_{between}$

$df_{total} = N - 1$

$df_{total} = {72} - 1$

$df_{total} = {71}$


$df_{between} = k - 1$

$df_{between} = {9} - 1$

$df_{between} = {8}$


$df_{within} = N - K$

$df_{within} = {72} - {9}$

$df_{within} = {63}$


Calculate the Sum of Squares: $SS_{total}, SS_{within}, \text{ and } SS_{between}$

$SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}$

$SS_{total} = {53741} - \frac{1935^2}{72}$

$SS_{total} = {53741} - \frac{3744225}{72}$

$SS_{total} = {53741} - {52003.12}$

$SS_{total} = {1737.88}$


$SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}$

$SS_{within} = {38.0 + 94.88 + 86.0 + 34.88 + 57.88 + 49.5 + 34.0 + 61.88 + 28.88}$

$SS_{within} = {485.9}$


$SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}$

$SS_{between} = {1737.88} - {485.9}$

$SS_{between} = {1251.98}$


note: the other way to calculate $SS_{betwen}$ is:

$SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}$




Stage 2 calculations for $Factor_A$

Summary Data for Factor_A
$T_{A_1} = {585} \quad n_{A_1} = {24}$
$T_{A_2} = {756} \quad n_{A_2} = {24}$
$T_{A_3} = {594} \quad n_{A_3} = {24}$

State the Hypotheses
$H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3$
$H_1:$ At least one mean is different

partition df for $Factor_A$

$df_{Factor_A} = k_{Factor_A} - 1$

$df_{Factor_A} = {3} - 1$

$df_{Factor_A} = {2}$


The decision criteria:

$F_{crit} = {4.96}, \alpha = {0.01}$

Partition the SS for $Factor_A$

Calculate $SS_{Factor_A}$

$\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_A}}^2}{n_{Factor_A}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}$

$SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{585}^2}{24} + \frac{{756}^2}{24} + \frac{{594}^2}{24}} - \frac{{1935}^2}{72}$

$SS_{Factor_A} = {\frac{{342225}}{24} + \frac{{571536}}{24} + \frac{{352836}}{24}} - \frac{{3744225}}{72}$

$SS_{Factor_A} = {{14259.38} + {23814.0} + {14701.5}} - {52003.12}$

$SS_{Factor_A} = {52774.88} - {52003.12}$

$SS_{Factor_A} = {771.76}$

Mean Squares for ${Factor_A}$

$MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{SS_{Factor_A}}{df_{Factor_A}}$

$MS_{Factor_A} = \frac{771.76}{2}$

$MS_{Factor_A} = {385.88}$

Calculate the F-Ratio for ${Factor_A}$

$F_{Factor_A} = \frac{MS_{Factor_A}}{MS_{Within}}$

$F_{Factor_A} = \frac{385.88}{7.71}$

$F_{Factor_A} = {50.05}$


Calculate $\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_A$

$\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_A}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_B}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}$

$\eta_p^2 = \frac{771.76}{{1737.88} - {2.34} - {477.88}}$

$\eta_p^2 = \frac{771.76}{1257.66}$

$\eta_p^2 = {0.61}$

The results:

reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,

$F({2}, {63}) = {50.05}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.61}$

Stage 2 calculations for $Factor_B$

Summary Data for Factor_B
$T_{B_1} = {641} \quad n_{B_1} = {24}$
$T_{B_2} = {643} \quad n_{B_2} = {24}$

$H_0: \mu_1 = \mu_2 = \mu_3$
$H_1:$ At least one mean is different

partition df for $Factor_B$

$df_{Factor_B} = {3} - 1$

$df_{Factor_B} = {2}$


The decision criteria:

$F_{crit} = {4.96}, \alpha = {0.01}$

Partition the SS for $Factor_B$

Calculate $SS_{Factor_B}$

$\Sigma{\frac{{T_{Factor_B}}^2}{n_{Factor_B}}} - \frac{G^2}{N}$

$SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{641}^2}{24} + \frac{{643}^2}{24} + \frac{{651}^2}{24}} - \frac{{1935}^2}{72}$

$SS_{Factor_B} = {\frac{{410881}}{24} + \frac{{413449}}{24} + \frac{{423801}}{24}} - \frac{{3744225}}{72}$

$SS_{Factor_B} = {{17120.04} + {17227.04} + {17658.38}} - {52003.12}$

$SS_{Factor_B} = {52005.46} - {52003.12}$

$SS_{Factor_B} = {2.34}$

Mean Squares for ${Factor_B}$

$MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{SS_{Factor_B}}{df_{Factor_B}}$

$MS_{Factor_B} = \frac{2.34}{2}$

$MS_{Factor_B} = {1.17}$

Calculate the F-Ratio for ${Factor_B}$

$F_{Factor_B} = \frac{MS_{Factor_B}}{MS_{Within}}$

$F_{Factor_B} = \frac{1.17}{7.71}$

$F_{Factor_B} = {0.15}$


Calculate $\eta_p^2 \text{ for } Factor_B$

$\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{Factor_B}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{AxB}}}}$

$\eta_p^2 = \frac{2.34}{{1737.88} - {771.76} - {477.88}}$

$\eta_p^2 = \frac{2.34}{488.24}$

$\eta_p^2 = {0.0}$

The results:

fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,

$F({2}, {63}) = {0.15}, p > {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.0}$

Stage 2 calculations for the $AxB$ interaction


State the Hypotheses
$H_0:$ There is no AxB interation
$H_1:$ There is an AxB interaction

Partition df for the interaction

$df_{AxB} = df_{Between} -df_{Factor_A} - df_{Factor_B}$

$df_{AxB} = {8} - {2} - {2}$

$df_{AxB} = {4}$



The decision criteria:

$F_{crit} = {3.63}, \alpha = {0.01}$

Partition the SS for $AxB$

Calculate $SS_{AxB}$

$SS_{{AxB}} = SS_{{Between}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}$

$SS_{AxB} = {1251.98} - {771.76} - {2.34}$

$SS_{AxB} = {477.88}$

Mean Squares for ${AxB}$

$MS_{AxB} = \frac{SS_{AxB}}{df_{AxB}}$

$MS_{AxB} = \frac{477.88}{4}$

$MS_{AxB} = {119.47}$

Calculate the F-Ratio for ${AxB}$

$F_{AxB} = \frac{MS_{AxB}}{MS_{Within}}$

$F_{AxB} = \frac{119.47}{7.71}$

$F_{AxB} = {15.5}$


Calculate $\eta_p^2 \text{ for } AxB$

$\eta_p^2 = \frac{{SS_{{AxB}}}}{{SS_{{Total}} - SS_{{Factor_A}} - SS_{{Factor_B}}}}$

$\eta_p^2 = \frac{477.88}{{1737.88} - {771.76} - {2.34}}$

$\eta_p^2 = \frac{477.88}{963.78}$

$\eta_p^2 = {0.5}$

The results:

reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,

$F({4}, {63}) = {15.5}, p < {0.01}, \eta_p^2 = {0.5}$ # Problem 2 Given the following data, use a use a 2-Factor ANOVA with $\alpha = {0.01}$ to analyze the following data
